114895.explanation: Haaksbergen has had the referendum instrument for about twenty years. That is a good thing. It is important to modernize the rules.

114898.explanation: 'Always' allows no choice. Wherever possible and feasible, the municipality should facilitate this.

114899.explanation: For obtaining subsidies, national schemes can be applied for.

114902.explanation: Greenery must be maintained throughout the municipality; replanting is only necessary when many trees have been cut down.

114905.explanation: A show should be accessible to everyone with various activities. Such a New Year's Eve can strengthen community spirit.

114906.explanation: Free collection can prevent littering or illegal dumping of waste.

114908.explanation: This is the responsibility of the building’s owner.

114909.explanation: Of course, within the applicable rules.

114910.explanation: News, developments, and announcements of events must be brought to attention.

114914.explanation: This should be a stepping stone towards more police presence on the streets.

114915.explanation: This is especially needed in the center of Haaksbergen.

114916.explanation: Enschede has a regional function in this.

114919.explanation: This relieves pressure on the housing market: it increases the supply in the housing market.

114920.explanation: However, consideration must be given to landscape values, agricultural businesses, and nature.

114921.explanation: This can be a stepping stone to a paid job.

114923.explanation: At the moment, this is already well arranged in Haaksbergen.

114926.explanation: The CDA stands for good and adequate care, where every second can count.

114929.explanation: Haaksbergen has a lot of nature and good facilities. That costs money.

114930.explanation: The expansion of Stepelerveld along the N18 is a good start.

114932.explanation: If people qualify for social rental housing, no discrimination may be made.

114935.explanation: The swimming diploma is a basic need for children. Swimming education is a supplement to physical education.

114936.explanation: From a cultural-historical perspective, it is nice to initiate this.

114937.explanation: There are regional agreements about this: coffeeshops can be established in Enschede. However, we are opponents of drug use.

114941.explanation: The neighborhood function can be realized, for example, in primary schools. This is already happening in some places.

114942.explanation: The shopping area must be strengthened. In a car-free center, there is more space for terraces.

114895.explanation: However, the subject must be suitable for a referendum. In March, simultaneous with the municipal elections, the first referendum in the history of Haaksbergen will take place.

114898.explanation: Always is a utopia that creates the impression that there are no conditions attached. If there are good conditions, then we agree with the statement. In itself, freedom is not bad.

114899.explanation: It depends on the case. It cannot be that a small group of residents benefits from a subsidy that is then paid by all residents of the municipality.

114902.explanation: This is easy to realize and is already happening. It can also be done in another location.

114905.explanation: If consumer fireworks are allowed, the municipality can facilitate or subsidize this. In fact, it is not a municipal task, so organizing it ourselves is not an option.

114906.explanation: If this means that bulky waste that does not fit in the gray container must be collected, then this is negotiable. If it means the current gray container, then this is not feasible. People may not pay for waste, but the money must come from somewhere.

114908.explanation: The municipality can encourage this or help in other ways, but it is also the responsibility of the entrepreneur themselves. These go hand in hand.

114909.explanation: The municipality must maintain control here by setting rules. Otherwise, situations could arise where neighbors (other farmers) are locked out due to the distance to nuisance, noise, and odor zones.

114910.explanation: A quality local broadcaster is important for villages like Haaksbergen, Buurse, and de Hoeve.

114914.explanation: Safety is primarily the responsibility of the police. The Mayor has influence here. Under certain circumstances, BOAs can assist.

114915.explanation: Camera surveillance is a measure experienced as intrusive by many residents. We should only apply it in places where it is really necessary and where there are no alternative methods to ensure safety.

114916.explanation: The important thing is that there are enough quality domestic violence shelters available. That can be in Haaksbergen. At this moment, there are already enough of these shelter places available regionally. This is organized on a regional basis.

114919.explanation: Basically not. However, under certain circumstances, yes. The conditions must be stable and clear enough for residents to know what they can rely on. Too much changing policy is bad for trust in the government.

114920.explanation: The housing task for Haaksbergen is so large that it cannot be done only within the built-up area. Therefore, a mix between inside and outside the built-up area is necessary. The community life and our facilities need an expansion of the number of houses (and thus residents).

114921.explanation: It is questionable whether you can impose this yourself as a municipality. However, the principle is good. If people have no work but can make another meaningful contribution to society, then that should be possible. Provided it is feasible.

114923.explanation: Haaksbergen already applies 15% above the national average. With that, we do more than is asked of us. Based on good information, we are willing to adjust this percentage.

114926.explanation: Provided the rates are within the same range. Let's not forget that most care in the Netherlands is organized regionally or nationally. The municipality has minimal to no influence on that.

114929.explanation: The tourist tax must fit what Haaksbergen has to offer. Careful consideration can be made each year about the level of the tourist tax.

114930.explanation: There is an opportunity here for the future. With the arrival of the new N18, Haaksbergen will be within reach of highways.

114932.explanation: Priority should be considered case by case and not be automatic. There must be a realistic mix in the allocation of social rental housing.

114935.explanation: School swimming contributes to the swimming skills of young residents. In addition, it contributes to a fun variation between theory and practical education.

114936.explanation: The history of Twents is quite interesting to share with students. However, teaching Twents is an old-fashioned idea. Current residents under 40 hardly speak Twents anymore.

114937.explanation: This does not fit with the business climate in Haaksbergen. Moreover, it has been shown nationally that this increases crime. Adjacent municipalities have enough opportunities to obtain such products.

114939.explanation: What is high? We interpret this as 5 floors or more. Few areas in Haaksbergen are suitable for this. However, Haaksbergen should allow high-rise buildings up to 5 floors where possible.

114941.explanation: Define neighborhood center? If a community center is meant here, then we say no. If a number of shops are meant, then we are in favor.

114942.explanation: For parts of the center, it can be considered whether it can be car-free. This is a careful process in which the needs of both residents and entrepreneurs are surveyed.

114946.explanation: The referendum will then be about the way Haaksbergen implements the national law. We cannot choose whether refugees come, but we can decide under what conditions these people are housed. Involving residents in such a topic is logical.

114895.explanation: Haaksbergen already has the possibility of an advisory referendum so that large groups can request a referendum.

114898.explanation: Not automatically. It must always be considered whether it fits in the environment. Shops must also meet legal requirements.

114899.explanation: Energy matters should be managed by the national government to avoid arbitrariness.

114902.explanation: Logical because trees are essential for nature, climate, health, and livability. It structurally prevents green loss and protects the quality of our living environment, now and for future generations.

114905.explanation: If there is a national fireworks ban, the municipality can facilitate that.

114906.explanation: There is already a scheme to collect bulky waste from homes. Free waste is no longer appropriate.

114908.explanation: After the sidewalk, that is a responsibility that belongs to entrepreneurs.

114909.explanation: House building must be regulated and remain so. As long as they continue farming actively, it cannot happen. If farmers completely stop their business, a new situation arises and there are already possibilities for that.

114910.explanation: Subsidies are already provided. According to the subsidy regulation, it is possible to submit a well-founded request that can be assessed. Furthermore, the local broadcaster can also generate income in other ways.

114914.explanation: Safety is a major focus in Haaksbergen and the surrounding villages. Residents have the right to feel safe at all times.

114915.explanation: As long as it promotes safety and does not harm the GDPR. The question is, who manages and controls that? So that process must be approached very carefully.

114916.explanation: To guarantee anonymity, you need to facilitate that in the big cities.

114919.explanation: It then no longer falls under the holiday home regulations, which means that holiday homes can be used as permanent residences at will.

114920.explanation: Infill (building within existing boundaries) takes precedence (legally) over expansion. So first use what is possible within village boundaries, then look outside but definitely not at the expense of our beautiful nature and splendid essen landscapes. It must fit the environment.

114921.explanation: Agreed, as long as it does not conflict with a psychological or physical disability.

114923.explanation: The income limit in Haaksbergen is already 115% of the social assistance standard. Additionally, there is the Stadsbank. Poverty is not necessarily income-dependent but can also relate to other situations. For example, debts, bankruptcy, etc., and people who just fall between the cracks.

114926.explanation: The quality of care provision is paramount, taking preferences into account, because with freedom of choice you respect someone's identity and conviction.

114929.explanation: TeamDAP does not want an increase in taxes.

114930.explanation: We already have sufficient industrial estates.

114932.explanation: Everyone is equal, so there should be no distinction between a status holder and a resident.

114935.explanation: Water safety is important and being able to swim is a basic need. The government obligation has been removed. Schools themselves are less inclined to reintroduce this requirement. This can be arranged by parents themselves.

114936.explanation: Not the municipality's responsibility but that of the schools.

114937.explanation: There is a lot of illegal trade and the step for young people, often also soft drugs of poor quality, to hard drugs is relatively small. With a coffeeshop you create social control and also more quality and safety as the number of places where illegal dealing occurs will decrease.

114939.explanation: This is already regulated in the current legislation.

114941.explanation: This does not have to be standard. Contact between residents can also take place in other places and in other ways (associations).

114946.explanation: If a large group of residents wishes so, they can already make use of this possibility.

114895.explanation: Haaksbergen has a referendum law. This allows residents to express their opinion. We want to preserve this referendum law.

114898.explanation: Tourism is a source of income for Haaksbergen. Also for more and more farmers. We want to give those farmers space.

114899.explanation: Currently, residents and energy cooperatives already receive high subsidies from the national government for generating sustainable energy. Think of the SDE subsidy. The municipality should be cautious when the national government already grants subsidies for certain sustainable investments.

114902.explanation: The VVD is a supporter of more greenery and more trees. This is important for livability, health, and protection against heat stress and water nuisance.

114905.explanation: Currently, there is uncertainty about the national fireworks policy. We want to keep ornamental fireworks. These can be set off by consumers, by associations, or by the municipality. The municipality must be open to residents who want to set off fireworks in an association.

114906.explanation: Unfortunately, collecting bulky waste is not free. Waste must be collected and incinerated. This costs the municipality money. If the municipality chooses to make bulky waste collection free, this will lead to higher waste disposal charges for all residents of Haaksbergen.

114908.explanation: We have not included anything about this topic in our election program. We will study this further in the coming period.

114909.explanation: This can be a good policy instrument to solve the housing shortage.

114910.explanation: Currently, the local broadcaster receives an annual subsidy. We want to keep the current subsidy as it is.

114914.explanation: We are in favor of more police on the streets. Additionally, we believe that civilian enforcement officers should focus more on safety.

114915.explanation: We want to deploy camera surveillance in places where nuisance is caused, for example by loitering youth.

114916.explanation: There must be enough places where victims of domestic violence can go. A women's shelter is a good example. Flexible housing for emergency seekers is, in our view, also a good solution for victims of domestic violence. We are open to both solutions.

114919.explanation: Allowing living in holiday homes can be a solution to the housing shortage. The municipality must handle this carefully and cautiously. For example, the infrastructure must be suitable.

114920.explanation: The VVD believes that 1800 new homes should be built in Haaksbergen. Of these, 900 should be built before 2030. The infill locations offer too little space for all these 1800 homes.

114921.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits must currently perform a mandatory counter-performance, provided they can. This can be work at, for example, a sports club. We want to keep this policy as it makes the step to paid employment easier.

114923.explanation: We find the current income limit for poverty assistance good. We do not intend to change this.

114926.explanation: If this leads to higher healthcare costs for the municipality without health benefits, the municipality does not have to go along with it in our view.

114929.explanation: We want to keep the tourist tax as it is now.

114930.explanation: It is important that companies get the opportunity to expand. This can prevent companies from leaving Haaksbergen. Additionally, expanding industrial estates creates new jobs.

114932.explanation: By giving refugees priority for social rental housing, it becomes more difficult for other housing seekers to find a home.

114935.explanation: We believe every child should have the opportunity to develop. School swimming is part of that.

114936.explanation: Primary schools have the freedom to decide whether they give attention to the Twents dialect in their education. The municipality will not interfere.

114937.explanation: Currently, the policy of the Twente region is that coffeeshops are only allowed in (large) cities. We find this current policy fine.

114939.explanation: Apartment buildings contribute to solving the housing shortage. From the responses at the participation evenings organized by the municipality, it emerged that 73 percent of residents believe flats may be built to address the housing shortage.

114941.explanation: We have no plans in our election program to increase the number of neighborhood centers. However, we strongly support preserving the current neighborhood and village centers, such as 'Het huis van Haaksbergen' and 'De trefkoel' in Buurse.

114942.explanation: For many people, the car is an important means of transport. Some people are less mobile and even dependent on the car.

114946.explanation: In our opinion, the municipality should not make irreversible decisions before the referendum has taken place. The referendum will be held on the same day as the municipal elections. After the referendum, the municipality must act on the outcome.

114895.explanation: Residents can express their opinions in many different ways. A referendum is an expensive way to gather opinions. Experience has shown that the result of a referendum does not work differently than making your voice heard in politics via consultation or taking the matter to court.

114898.explanation: The problem lies in the word 'always.' A shop or campsite can be an impediment to farmers/entrepreneurs in the area. If it fits spatially and economically, we are positive.

114905.explanation: Now that fireworks are banned, we must provide alternatives.

114906.explanation: We want this to be investigated first.

114908.explanation: We want to make buildings more accessible for people with disabilities. Financially supporting this goes too far for us.

114909.explanation: Farmers are struggling and we need housing. If it complies with the rules, it can be done.

114910.explanation: We do need to know what the money is being used for.

114914.explanation: Collaboration with police and justice is important.

114915.explanation: In the center, near the GAP, and other locations with nuisance, (temporary) camera surveillance may be installed.

114916.explanation: If there is (regional) demand for it.

114919.explanation: This leads to the disappearance of the tourism sector.

114920.explanation: Space is needed to build enough houses. However, we must be cautious with the Honesch.

114921.explanation: This completely depends on the situation.

114923.explanation: There is still a lot of 'silent poverty'.

114929.explanation: Tourist tax should be linked to expenditures for advertising Haaksbergen.

114930.explanation: If there is a need for more business parks, this is possible. For now, we have enough space with the development of ‘t Stepelerveld.

114932.explanation: These are also residents of Haaksbergen.

114936.explanation: Really great, if it's in Twents dialect.

114937.explanation: The current agreements in Twente are good.

114939.explanation: Tall apartment buildings do not fit with the villages Haaksbergen, de Hoeve, and Buurse.

114941.explanation: With the Trefkoel, ‘t Meuke, and the Kappen, we already have nice centers.

114946.explanation: The decision has already been made.

114895.explanation: D66 supports citizen involvement. The referendum is one means to organize this. D66 also supports other methods to increase involvement.

114898.explanation: D66 is open to such initiatives.

114899.explanation: The municipality should encourage sustainability initiatives. This does not have to be financial support only but can also be other forms of support.

114902.explanation: D66 stands for a green living environment and wants at least one tree replanted for every tree felled.

114905.explanation: Now that the fireworks ban has come into effect, it is good to offer an alternative. This is not a municipal task, but we support initiatives from residents.

114906.explanation: D66 stands for the reuse of materials. We see an important role for Twente Milieu and our recycling center. Collecting waste from homes can reduce dumping in rural areas.

114908.explanation: The municipality should promote good accessibility to public facilities. This does not have to be only financial aid but can also include other types of support.

114909.explanation: This is only possible under the red-for-red scheme and not for the agricultural development area (between Sint Isidorushoeve and Hengevelde).

114910.explanation: The municipality continues to use the compensation from the municipal fund for the local broadcaster. Occasionally, extra funds can be made available for activities.

114914.explanation: Enforcement during the day is generally sufficient. The exceptions are in the evenings and on weekends.

114915.explanation: Safety is important, but more camera surveillance is not the first solution.

114916.explanation: D66 pays a lot of attention to violence against women. A women’s shelter must be available in the region, but not necessarily in Haaksbergen.

114919.explanation: We must pull out all the stops to address the housing shortage. We should also be more flexible with these rules, although customization is required for each home.

114920.explanation: We must pull out all the stops in the fight against the housing shortage. We need to look at both infill and expansion locations.

114921.explanation: We encourage meaningful work, but the social assistance benefit is a right, so we cannot make this mandatory.

114923.explanation: Partly at D66’s request, the income limit for receiving aid for poverty has been raised. This is currently at a good level.

114926.explanation: We must support this as much as possible and provide tailored solutions where feasible.

114929.explanation: The tourist tax is currently at a good level.

114930.explanation: There is still demand for more business parks in Haaksbergen. We choose to expand at Stepelerveld with access via Kolenbranderweg.

114932.explanation: The municipality is legally required to accommodate status holders.

114935.explanation: Swimming is a basic skill. We do not support introducing school swimming. However, the municipality should financially support parents who cannot afford swimming lessons (through the Stic Leergeld foundation).

114936.explanation: Schools themselves determine the content of their educational programs.

114937.explanation: D66 nationally supports the legalization of soft drugs. A coffee shop should be available in the region, but not necessarily in Haaksbergen.

114939.explanation: We must pull out all the stops in the fight against the housing shortage.

114941.explanation: This certainly applies to our village centers Sint Isidorushoeve and Buurse. For the other neighborhoods, we can consider clustering such a facility.

114942.explanation: D66 wants pedestrians and cyclists to always be given priority in the design of areas, with cars coming afterward. Disabled persons must still have access by car.

114946.explanation: There will be a referendum on March 18. D66 values citizen participation in municipal council decisions. Although the referendum is advisory, we will consider the result very carefully.

statements.title: Before Haaksbergen decides to accept refugees, a referendum must be held.

titleParts.text: Voor Haaksbergen besluit om vluchtelingen op te vangen moet er een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: In Haaksbergen there should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
There should be more CCTV surveillance in Haaksbergen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Haaksbergen should have more business parks/industrial estates.
The center of Haaksbergen should become car-free.
Haaksbergen should have a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: More high-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Haaksbergen.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
Residents of Haaksbergen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Haaksbergen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to Twents.
